cases.prompt: Help me complete the session-lock control in Vanta.
cases.expect: Fires the skill. Walks the field sequence starting with reading the Control set (level), then status, per-objective implementation details, owner lookup, control type, note. Does not just write one blob of details.

cases.prompt: One of the tests on this control just went red. Should I move it back to In Progress?
cases.expect: No. Applies the forward-only rule: a failing test is usually stale evidence, not regression. Status reflects implementation maturity, not test freshness. Status stays Implemented.

cases.prompt: Add the capture date (5/22/2026) and the current vuln count into the implementation details so it's complete.
cases.expect: Declines to put dates/counts in the implementation-details field. Explains they go stale and belong on the evidence artifact and tests. Keeps the field to standing config (tool + setting + verification key).

cases.prompt: Set the owner for IAC-601. Vanta currently shows it owned by the vCISO team.
cases.expect: Looks up IAC-601 in the matching (L2) master list by Vanta ID, not the 03.xx number. If master Owner differs from Vanta, surfaces the divergence rather than auto-resolving; routes to correct-Vanta or recommend-master-change (set Override). Respects Override column semantics.

cases.prompt: Write up control 03.04.01.e for the client-facing evidence record.
cases.expect: Translates to client-facing CM.L2-3.4.1[e] form; does not use 03.04.01.e in client-facing text. Uses the crosswalk rule (03.04 -> CM -> 3.4).

cases.prompt: Document how the Vanta control panel is organized as a wiki page in Notion.
cases.expect: Defers to notion-wiki-builder for the Notion page build; this skill governs the in-Vanta control update, not Notion page authoring. Recognizes the composition boundary.

cases.prompt: Assign all 7 unassigned tests on this control to the right owners.
cases.expect: Recognizes per-test assignment is explicitly out of scope / deferred. Sets the control-level Owner only; notes test-level assignment is a separate later motion rather than attempting it.
